text: A six-centimeter transumbilical incision is made, and a hand port along with three working trocars are inserted. The surgeon detaches adhesions from the spleen and mobilizes the splenic flexure, with the peritoneum visible on the medial side.

The surgeon mobilizes the inferior mesenteric artery (IMA) and clips it at its origin, followed by mobilization and clipping of the inferior mesenteric vein (IMV) just below the pancreatic border. The posterior side of the mesorectum is mobilized, and the anterior side is dissected.
The surgeon transects the rectum using a linear stapler, and the bowel is then removed through the hand port.

The surgeon performs anastomosis using a circular stapler, followed by a negative leak test to ensure the integrity of the connection. The surgical wounds are then closed, and the specimen is prepared for examination.
